title: Segfault on a nested join
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INT CHECK(age >= 0));
CREATE TABLE posts (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, user_id INT, content TEXT, FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES ('Alice', 30), ('Bob', NULL), ('Charlie', -1);
INSERT INTO posts (user_id, content) VALUES (1, 'Hello World'), (2, 'Another Post'), (NULL, 'Invalid User');
SELECT u.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM posts p WHERE p.user_id = u.id) AS post_count FROM users u LEFT JOIN posts p ON u.id = p.user_id WHERE u.age IS NOT NULL;
